summary:
An individual on a fat-free carbohydrate-rich diet will have increased triglyceride levels due to excess carbs converting into fatty acids; thus, very-low-density lipoprotein (VLDL) is most likely to elevate as it transports these extra fatty acids. Agent1 concluded based on dietary effects on lipid metabolism that B represents the answer for which lipoprotein was probably raised given such conditions. 

Note: Ensure "B" corresponds correctly with 'very-low density lipoprotein' or another specific term if provided within options not shown here directly but implied by the response structure requested above.
